chunk_summary: The discharge nutrition bundle template ensures standardized documentation for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants, covering feeding plans, comorbidities, intake prescriptions, micronutrient supplementation, and follow-up care to mitigate risks post-NICU discharge.
learning_objectives: Understand the components of a discharge nutrition bundle for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants.
Learn how to document feeding phenotypes, comorbidities, and observed feeding assessments.
Identify key micronutrient supplementation guidelines and follow-up care protocols.
key_facts: Minimum documentation fields include GA at birth, birth weight, discharge weight, and corrected age.
Feeding phenotypes include direct breastfeeding, measurable top-ups, expressed milk feeds, formula/mixed, or tube feeds.
Micronutrient supplementation includes vitamin D (IU/day) and iron (mg/kg/day elemental).
Fortification methods include HMF in EBM or nutrient-enriched formula with specific recipes and limits.
Follow-up booking should occur within 24–72 hours post-discharge for Tier 3 care.
reference_quote: Transitioning the breastfeeding preterm infant from the NICU to home requires careful planning and documentation to ensure feeding safety and nutritional adequacy. (Academy of Breastfeeding Medicine Clinical Protocol #12)
document_key_topics: Standardized discharge nutrition documentation for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants
Follow-up care and caregiver monitoring tools
